verdict_summary: HAR captured. /v2/chat returns 200 with V1-schema SSE (event_type=stream-start/text-generation/stream-end) but the Cohere V2 playground UI expects V2-schema discriminant 'type' (e.g. content-delta) and fails to render the streamed APF warning with error '[error] response: Missing discriminant ("type")'.

evidence.type_discriminant: event_type (V1 schema actually emitted) -- V2 playground UI expects 'type' (NOT present)

notes: Key V2 schema findings: (1) Network shows POST https://api.cohere.com/v2/chat with Accept: text/event-stream, response Content-Type text/event-stream, status 200. (2) SSE format uses named SSE events (event: stream-start / text-generation / stream-end) with JSON data payload. (3) The actual JSON data uses discriminant field 'event_type' with values stream-start | text-generation | stream-end, content path data.text. (4) Cohere V2 playground frontend rejects this with '[error] response: Missing discriminant ("type")' --- it expects the V2 unified-chat schema (type=message-start/content-delta/etc. with delta.message.content[].text). (5) APF block was triggered: backend correctly streamed the warning text '⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n\nThis request has been blocked due to sensitive information detected.' but UI rendered an error instead. Schema-mismatch makes V1 warning chunks unusable in the V2 playground -- warning impl for cohere must emit V2-schema chunks (type discriminant + delta.message.content.text path) to render correctly. Model: command-a-03-2025. Request body: {messages:[{role:user,content:[{type:text,text:...}]}], temperature:0.3, model:..., stream:true}.
